add_static_peer_note: Creates a static peer in desired config. Dynamic IX members still come from membership admission.
advanced_json_available: Raw JSON editor is below the visual config.
admin_proof_note: The private key stays in this browser session and is used only to sign API requests.
admin_proof_restored: Admin proof restored for this browser tab
domain_ix_note: Live T1 IX counts include this IX and directly observed IX members; downstream IX are shown separately.
domain_prefixes_note: Accepted unique runtime prefixes visible to this IX, grouped by route source.
domain_route_prefixes_note: Runtime prefixes known to this IX, including direct peers and downstream transit LANs.
route_candidates_note: Accepted and rejected candidate paths before the runtime table picks the best route for each prefix.
links_note: Per-IX transport sessions, routes, and datapath health.
name: Name
runtime_routes_note: Read-only routes currently selected by the control plane. Sources can include local LANs, devices, static config, or peer advertisements.
unlock_body: Select an Admin certificate and private key before the WebUI can read or change the API.
help_domain_id: Domain identifier shared by IX nodes that belong to the same TrustIX domain.
help_ix_id: Unique name of this IX node inside the domain. Peers and routes use this value as the next hop.
help_ix_control_api: URL other IX nodes use to call this node's control API, usually https://host:port.
help_ix_control_api_publish: auto publishes ix.control_api, or falls back to the peer API listen address when empty. disabled is for edge IX nodes without public inbound control-plane access.
help_ix_cert: Path to the IX certificate used for node identity, trust propagation, and optional management TLS.
help_ix_key: Path to the private key matching the IX certificate.
help_lan_iface: LAN interface handled by TrustIX. Use an existing LAN NIC or the virtual interface name.
help_lan_underlay_iface: Physical or underlay interface used for transport traffic and kernel datapath attachment.
help_lan_gateway: Gateway address exposed to local LAN hosts when TrustIX manages LAN addressing.
help_lan_mode: routed advertises LAN prefixes directly; nat rewrites LAN traffic behind this IX.
help_lan_attach_mode: managed lets TrustIX create/manage the interface; existing attaches to an interface already present on the host.
help_lan_nat_max_bindings: Maximum active NAT bindings for LAN traffic. Leave 0 to use the default.
help_lan_nat_binding_ttl: Idle lifetime for NAT bindings, such as 5m or 1h.
help_lan_advertise: CIDR prefixes this IX announces to other IX nodes, one prefix per line.
help_lan_manage_address: Allow TrustIX to add or update addresses on the LAN interface.
help_lan_id: Stable LAN identifier used by primary_lan_id and diagnostics.
help_lan_type: local is a private LAN behind this IX; trusted_public is a directly reachable trusted network segment.
help_lan_manage_forwarding: Allow TrustIX to enable required Linux forwarding sysctls.
help_lan_manage_rp_filter: Allow TrustIX to adjust rp_filter to avoid asymmetric route drops.
help_management_tls_mode: Controls HTTPS for the API and WebUI: auto uses TLS when credentials are available, required refuses plaintext, disabled allows HTTP.
help_management_tls_identity: Choose whether management TLS uses the IX certificate or a custom certificate/key pair.
help_management_tls_cert: Custom management TLS certificate path when TLS identity is custom_cert.
help_management_tls_key: Private key path for the custom management TLS certificate.
help_management_web_ui_enabled: Expose the embedded WebUI on the same listener as the API.
help_management_host_api_enabled: Enable the local host API used by LAN/localhost clients.
help_management_host_api_listen: Listen address for the host API, for example 127.0.0.1:8787 or 0.0.0.0:8787.
help_management_host_api_require_read_au: Require Admin proof even for read-only API calls.
help_transport_policy_mode: Overall transport preference, such as auto or a specific policy mode.
help_transport_policy_encryption: Default encryption direction. secure encrypts both directions; plaintext disables transport encryption.
help_transport_policy_kernel_transport_m: Controls whether UDP/TCP/GRE/IPIP datapath should prefer or require the kernel fast path.
help_transport_policy_crypto_placement: Where payload encryption runs: auto, userspace fallback, or kernel when supported.
help_transport_policy_crypto_key_source: Key derivation source for transport encryption, such as TrustIX X25519 or TLS exporter.
help_transport_policy_mtu: Datapath MTU. Leave 0 to let TrustIX choose from the interface and transport.
help_transport_policy_fragment_policy: Whether fragmented packets are accepted or dropped before forwarding.
help_transport_policy_session_pool_size: Number of parallel transport sessions to maintain for load distribution.
help_transport_policy_session_pool_strat: How packets choose a pooled session: by flow, five tuple, packet, or round robin.
help_transport_policy_candidates: Preferred local endpoint names for outgoing sessions, one per line.
help_transport_policy_crypto_suites: Allowed crypto suites in preference order, one per line.
help_endpoint_name: Local endpoint name referenced by peers, routes, and transport policies.
help_endpoint_transport: Underlying transport used by this endpoint, such as udp, experimental_tcp, gre, or ipip.
help_endpoint_mode: passive listens for inbound sessions; active dials the peer address.
help_endpoint_listen: Local listen address for passive endpoints, for example 0.0.0.0:4433.
help_endpoint_address: Remote address dialed by active endpoints.
help_tunnel_endpoint_listen: Local GRE/IPIP/VXLAN capability declaration. Configure this node's underlay and optional hints; peer and carrier values are negotiated by the control plane.
help_tunnel_endpoint_address: Local GRE/IPIP/VXLAN capability declaration. Peer underlay, carrier addresses, and default VNI are derived from peer advertisements and control-plane negotiation.
help_tunnel_local_underlay: This node's public or underlay IPv4 address. Peer address, carrier, VNI, and MTU are negotiated by the control plane per IX link.
help_tunnel_underlay_if: Optional Linux underlay interface name for VXLAN or tunnel creation.
help_peer_tunnel_address: Static tunnel address. Dynamic GRE/IPIP/VXLAN endpoints come from IX member advertisements and are negotiated locally.
help_endpoint_security_link_tls: TLS behavior for the transport link before payload encryption decisions.
help_endpoint_security_encryption: Endpoint-level encryption override for this transport.
help_publish_mode: Controls which IX peers can see this local endpoint in control-plane advertisements.
help_publish_only_peers: IX IDs allowed to see this endpoint, one per line. Used with allowlist.
help_publish_except_peers: IX IDs that must not see this endpoint, one per line. Used with denylist.
help_endpoint_security_crypto_suites: Comma-separated crypto suites allowed on this endpoint.
help_endpoint_enabled: Enable or disable this endpoint without deleting its configuration.
help_peer_id: IX ID of the remote peer node.
help_peer_domain: Domain ID expected from the remote peer. Leave aligned with this domain unless intentionally bridging.
help_peer_control_api: Remote peer control API URL used for sync, health, and management operations.
help_peer_allowed_prefixes: Prefixes this peer is allowed to advertise or receive, one CIDR per line.
help_peer_peer_endpoints: Remote endpoints in name, address, transport, mode format; one endpoint per line.
help_route_kind: Route behavior: unicast forwards, local terminates, blackhole drops silently, reject returns an error.
help_route_next_hop: IX ID used as the next hop for this route.
help_route_endpoint: Endpoint name preferred for this route.
help_route_policy: Optional policy name or tag used by advanced routing rules.
help_route_metric: Lower metrics win when multiple routes match the same prefix.
transit_route_hint: Transit route: this prefix belongs to the owner IX, and packets are sent to the next-hop IX first.
endpoint_probe_missing: Select a peer endpoint to test
help_endpoint_priority: Integer exchanged during endpoint negotiation. Transport profile/datapath pick the endpoint class first; higher combined priority breaks ties within comparable endpoints.
help_endpoint_transport_profile: Endpoint-level profile override advertised to peers.
help_profile_features: Low-level transport capability flags advertised to peers. Leave empty unless a specific transport implementation requires them.
help_transport_datapath: Preferred datapath for this transport profile.
help_transport_profile: Default transport behavior profile. Use performance only after both IX endpoints support the advertised features.
peer_endpoints_readonly_note: Read-only; learned from peer advertisements or static desired config.
peer_endpoints_runtime_note: Learned from peer advertisements and live negotiation.
transport_advanced_note: Optional performance gates for batching, GSO/GRO, and large frames.
kernel_capabilities_note: Runtime module, TC/XDP/AF_XDP, crypto, and full-kernel datapath readiness.
access_note: Issue device certificates and endpoint grants from the WebUI.
grant_apply_required: Click Apply before runtime enforcement changes
grant_enforced_detail: Inbound data sessions require an active endpoint grant
grant_not_enforced: Grant is not enforced until this endpoint uses require_grant
help_access_allowed_peers: IX IDs allowed without an endpoint grant, one per line.
help_access_mode: open accepts configured IX peers; require_grant also requires an active endpoint grant unless the peer is allowlisted.
help_bootstrap_routes: Optional static client routes written into the generated config. Normal route updates are sent by the IX after the device connects.
help_device_advertise_prefixes: CIDR prefixes this device or downstream IX is allowed to announce into the TrustIX domain. Use independent downstream LANs by default; use a more-specific subprefix of this IX LAN only when intentionally delegating that subnet to the device. The upstream IX forwards by authorization and does not let the device decide global routes. Leave empty for a single leased /32 only.
help_device_issue_endpoint: Select an enabled passive local endpoint. Its configured public address is used unless an override is set below.
help_grant_endpoint: Local passive endpoint this authorization applies to. Endpoints using require_grant are listed first.
help_grant_default_ttl: Default TTL used when issuing grants for this endpoint.
help_grant_subject_ix: Remote IX allowed to open inbound data sessions on the selected local endpoint.
help_join_new_ix: Issue a one-time provision token, then paste the generated command on the target Linux host. CA private keys stay on this IX/provisioner.
help_new_ix_api_listen: Management API listen address on the target IX. Keep it loopback unless you intend to expose the WebUI/API.
help_new_ix_attach_mode: Managed lets TrustIX create and manage the LAN interface; existing uses an interface already present on the target host.
help_new_ix_bootstrap_peer: Existing IX used as the first control-plane peer for the generated config.
help_new_ix_bootstrap_control_api: Control API URL of the bootstrap peer. Empty uses the current IX control API from this config.
help_new_ix_build_bpf: Whether the bootstrap build should rebuild embedded eBPF objects before deployment.
help_new_ix_build_ko: Whether the bootstrap build should build TrustIX kernel modules. Auto builds them when kernel headers are available.
help_new_ix_config_ca_cert: Config CA certificate path on the provisioner, used to verify the route authorization issuer.
help_new_ix_config_ca_key: Config CA private key path on the provisioner. It signs route authorizations and is not sent to the target.
help_new_ix_control_api: Optional public URL other IX nodes use to reach this new IX control API. Empty derives https://host:9443 from the public endpoint.
help_new_ix_control_api_active: Optional published control API for this edge IX. Leave empty when it has no public inbound control-plane address.
help_new_ix_dataplane: Runtime dataplane mode for the target IX. Auto chooses the best supported path; noop is only for diagnostics.
help_new_ix_domain_locked: Provisioned IX nodes always join the current TrustIX domain; the server rejects any different domain.
help_new_ix_domain_ca_cert: Domain CA certificate path on the provisioner. It identifies the TrustIX domain issuing the new IX certificate.
help_new_ix_domain_ca_key: Domain CA private key path on the provisioner. It is used only to sign the new IX certificate and is not sent to the target.
help_new_ix_endpoint_address: Public data endpoint address written into the new IX config. Domain:port and IP:port are both valid.
help_new_ix_endpoint_mode: Passive inbound publishes a reachable endpoint for peers to dial. Active outbound is for edge IX nodes without public inbound access.
help_new_ix_endpoint_listen: Optional listen address. Empty listens on 0.0.0.0 with the public endpoint port.
help_new_ix_endpoint_name: Optional local endpoint name written into the generated config. Empty derives <ix_id>-<transport>.
help_new_ix_goarch: Optional target CPU architecture for cross-builds. Empty uses the target host architecture when possible.
help_new_ix_id: Unique IX identity to issue. It must differ from the current IX and becomes the certificate IX name.
help_new_ix_kernel_modules: Controls whether target install may load TrustIX kernel modules. Required fails installation if modules cannot load.
help_new_ix_lan_gateway: Optional local gateway CIDR. Public/edge/lab roles derive the first address of the first advertised prefix; transit leaves it empty.
help_new_ix_lan_iface: Optional LAN interface. Managed mode creates trustix-<ix_id> when empty; existing mode requires a host interface.
help_new_ix_lan_prefixes: CIDR prefixes this IX owns or is authorized to transit, one per line. They become route authorizations, not device bootstrap routes.
help_new_ix_peer_api_listen: Peer control API listen address on the target IX, used by other IX nodes for control-plane sync.
help_new_ix_profile: Profile chooses stable, performance, latency, compatibility, or plaintext performance defaults.
help_new_ix_provision_url: Public management URL of this IX/provisioner. Empty uses the current WebUI origin seen by the server.
help_new_ix_provision_ttl: Lifetime of the one-time bootstrap token. Short TTLs reduce risk if the command is leaked.
help_new_ix_role: Role selects the generated LAN defaults and route-exchange intent.
help_new_ix_source_certs: Local directory on the provisioner that contains domain/config CA material used to issue the new IX.
help_new_ix_target: Optional user@host. Leave empty for a target-local command; set it to generate a management-host SSH deployment command.
help_new_ix_target_cert_dir: Directory path written into the generated target config for IX certificates and trust roots.
help_new_ix_trust_roots: Optional trust root paths, one per line. Empty uses certs/root-ca.pem, certs/domain-ca.pem, and certs/config-ca.pem if present.
help_new_ix_transport: Initial passive data transport for the new IX endpoint. UDP is the safest default; advanced deployments can choose TCP or tunnel transports.
help_new_ix_transport_active: Data transport used to dial the upstream endpoint. It should match the selected upstream endpoint transport.
help_new_ix_upstream_endpoint: Data endpoint on the upstream IX that this no-public-IP edge IX dials. Empty derives host:7000 from the bootstrap control API. It is not advertised as the new IX public address.
help_new_ix_underlay_iface: Optional physical or WAN interface hint used by kernel fast paths and tunnel transports.
help_public_endpoint_override: Optional address written into the generated client config instead of the selected endpoint address. Use domain:port or IP:port.
new_ix_active_requires_bootstrap_control: Active outbound IX requires a bootstrap control API
new_ix_command_pending: Issue a one-time token to generate the target command.
new_ix_required_fields: IX ID, data endpoint, and advertised prefixes are required
help_device_client_config_json: Device client config JSON. The quick-join command writes it on the target device and references the issued certificate and trust roots.
help_device_encryption: Encryption requirement for device connections to the upstream endpoint. secure uses authenticated encryption; plaintext is for isolated lab paths only.
help_device_id: Stable device identity written into the issued certificate and device access lease.
help_device_quick_join_command: One-command setup for the target device. It writes the certificate, private key, trust roots, and systemd service.
help_device_server_name: Optional TLS Server Name override. Set this when the endpoint uses a domain certificate or reverse proxy.
help_device_ttl: Lifetime for the device certificate and lease, for example 24h, 168h, or 30d.
help_endpoint_crypto_suites: Encryption suites allowed for this endpoint. Leave empty to use the default safe set.
help_endpoint_grant_reason: Note written into the grant audit record so the authorization purpose is clear later.
help_endpoint_grant_ttl: Lifetime for the endpoint grant, for example 1h or 24h. The peer needs a new grant after it expires.
help_endpoint_profile_crypto_placement: Endpoint-level crypto placement override. auto selects kernel or userspace from both sides' capabilities.
help_endpoint_profile_encryption: Endpoint-level encryption requirement override. secure is the safe default; plaintext is only for trusted lab links.
help_endpoint_publish_mode: Choose whether endpoint publishing inherits the global policy, publishes only to this IX, hides from this IX, or is fully disabled.
help_endpoint_publish_to_peer: Controls whether this local endpoint is advertised to the currently selected IX peer.
help_endpoint_transport_datapath: Endpoint-level datapath override. auto selects kernel module, TC/XDP, or userspace from available capability.
help_lan_device_access: Allow certificate-based devices to join this LAN.
help_lan_device_address_pool: CIDR pool used for device address leases. It should belong to this LAN or an explicitly authorized access segment.
help_lan_device_lease_ttl: How long device address leases are retained. Devices can renew while their certificate remains valid.
help_local_bind_iface: Linux interface name used for outbound dials with SO_BINDTODEVICE. Requires sufficient privileges.
help_local_bind_source_ip: Local underlay source IP used for outbound dials. Leave empty to let the OS route choose.
help_new_ix_bootstrap_command: One-time command to run on the target Linux host. It fetches the provision token payload without exposing CA private keys.
help_peer_ix: Static peer IX ID. Dynamic members are normally learned from admission and advertisements.
help_probe_destination: Destination used for health probing or route tracing, usually the peer LAN gateway.
help_promote_runtime_route: Copy this learned runtime route into desired static routes, then edit and apply it.
help_route_owner: IX that owns the destination prefix. For transit routes, owner is the downstream/destination IX and next hop is the immediate IX to send packets to.
help_selected_lan: Select which LAN entry to edit. The list can include the legacy LAN block and multi-LAN entries.
help_selected_peer: Select which static peer entry to edit. Runtime peers learned from the domain are shown separately.
help_session_warmup: Pre-open transport sessions for configured peers so the first data flow avoids dial latency.
help_transport_allow_checksum_skip: Allow checksum shortcuts only after validating the public path preserves packet correctness.
help_transport_allow_outer_gso_unsafe: Allow outer-packet GSO experimental paths that may depend on NIC and kernel behavior.
help_transport_allow_unsafe: Allow experimental fast paths marked unsafe. Keep disabled in production.
help_transport_batch_bytes: Maximum bytes gathered into one batch. Larger values improve throughput but can increase latency and memory peaks.
help_transport_flush_delay: Batch wait time before flushing. Shorter values reduce latency; longer values can improve throughput.
help_transport_gro: Allow GRO receive aggregation when both endpoints and the underlying kernel support it.
help_transport_gso: Allow GSO batched transmit when both endpoints and the underlying kernel support it.
help_transport_large_frames: Allow larger internal frames when both endpoints and the selected datapath support them.
help_transport_max_frames: Maximum frames per batch. Excessive values can increase tail latency and memory pressure.
help_transport_override_crypto_placement: Override crypto placement for a transport profile: auto, kernel, or userspace.
help_transport_override_datapath: Override datapath for a transport profile: auto, kernel_module, tc_xdp, or userspace.
help_transport_override_encryption: Override encryption requirement for a transport profile: secure or plaintext.
help_transport_override_profile: Transport profile name to override, such as stable, performance, or latency.
help_transport_override_transport: Underlying transport this override applies to. Empty applies to every transport in the profile.
help_transport_shards: Parallel send/receive shard count. More shards can increase throughput but also CPU and memory pressure.
help_tunnel_mtu: Tunnel interface MTU. Leave room for outer encapsulation headers; oversized values can cause fragmentation.
help_tunnel_port: UDP/TCP port used by tunnel transports.
help_tunnel_vni: VNI used by tunnel transports such as VXLAN to isolate multiple tunnels on one underlay.
